seoTitle: Plot Two Data Sets in One Graph in Google Sheets
metaDescription: Learn how to plot two data sets in a single Google Sheets graph to compare trends like sales versus marketing spend, all in one clear visual.
intro: Comparing two sets of numbers side by side is much easier with a single combined graph than with two separate charts. This guide walks through plotting two data sets in one graph in Google Sheets, a technique useful for comparing trends, tracking performance, or analyzing outcomes across different datasets.

steps.heading: Prepare your data
steps.body: Organize both data sets into clearly labeled columns so Sheets can plot them together without confusion.

steps.heading: Create the first graph
steps.body: Start by building a basic chart using your primary data set as the foundation for the comparison.

steps.heading: Add the second data set
steps.body: Bring in your second series so both sets of values appear on the same chart, making direct comparison possible.

steps.heading: Finalize your graph
steps.body: Adjust labels, colors, and axes so the combined chart is easy to read and clearly communicates the comparison you're making.

faq.question: Why would I want two data sets on one graph instead of two separate charts?
faq.answer: A single combined graph makes it much easier to visually compare trends, such as sales figures against marketing spend, side by side.

faq.question: Do both data sets need to use the same scale?
faq.answer: Not necessarily. Google Sheets allows for a secondary axis when the two data sets have very different value ranges, keeping both readable.

faq.question: Is this technique beginner-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the process is designed to be approachable even for those with little to no experience with data visualization in Google Sheets.

faq.question: What are common business use cases for this?
faq.answer: Businesses often use this to compare sales figures against marketing expenditures or track performance across two related metrics over time.
